source.text:
-- Effects and control: graded ops, handlers, masks, loops, state, errors.
effect Ask
  ask() : Int

effect Log
  never fail(String) : Int
  once emit(Int) : Unit

error Boom(String)

fn handled() : Int =
  handle ask() + ask() with
    ask() resume k => k(21)
    return r => r

fn sugar_arms() : Int =
  handle emit(ask()) with
    once ask() => 7
    never fail(msg) => 0
    val base = 5
    once emit(v) => ()
    return r => base()

fn named() : Int =
  with f <- handler
    ask() resume k => k(1)
    return r => r
  f.ask()

fn tunneled() : Int =
  handle mask<Ask>(ask()) with
    ask() resume k => k(2)
    return r => r

fn thrower(n : Int) : Int =
  if n < 0 then
    throw Boom("negative")
  else
    n

fn catcher() : Int = try thrower(-1) catch { Boom(msg) => 0 }

fn looping() : Int =
  var acc := 0
  var i := 0
  while i < 10 do
    i += 1
    if i % 2 == 0 then
      continue
    if i > 7 then
      break
    acc := acc + i
  for v in [1, 2, 3],
      if v > 1,
      let w = v * 2 do
    acc += w
  loop
    break
  acc

fn early(n : Int) : Int =
  if n == 0 then
    return 99
  n + 1

fn atomic() : Int =
  var balance := 100
  let r =
    transact
      balance -= 40
      guard(balance >= 80)
      1
    else
      0
  probe "trace" do println("attempted")
  balance

fn grid_write() : List(List(Int)) =
  var grid := [[0, 1], [2, 3]]
  grid[0][1] := 9
  grid
